systemLabel: Linux base system attached · systemd · wayland · online
walletLabel: Wallet bridge pending · ownership surface visible
runtimeSource: Rust runtime-core mediates between Linux host services and the SolOS operating layer.
runtimeRole: Intermediary layer between Linux base system and SolOS operating layer
mediationStatus: Host facts detected and normalized into SolOS-facing runtime state

home.summaryTitle: SolOS v1.0 runs above a runtime intermediary
home.summarySubtitle: Linux is the base system, the runtime mediates, and SolOS delivers the operating layer.
home.summaryBody: This build treats runtime-core as the middle layer between Linux and SolOS. It reads host state from Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS, kernel 7.0.0, hostname lcpby-HP-ENVY-TS-17-Notebook-PC, user lcpby, uptime 4d 22h 41m, and current session plumbing, then exposes that state as stable runtime context for the shell.
home.nextActionSubtitle: Promote mediation into real runtime services
home.nextActionBody: Wire approvals, app launch, wallet bridges, and agent actions through runtime-mediated services instead of leaving the shell coupled to static fixtures.

ghost.thesisLabel: SolOS should sit above a runtime intermediary that translates Linux host reality into stable operating-layer semantics.

quickActions.title: Inspect mediated host services
quickActions.subtitle: Runtime as intermediary
quickActions.description: Read systemd, session, network, and process state through the runtime layer instead of exposing raw host details everywhere in the shell.

quickActions.title: Bind approvals to mediated commands
quickActions.subtitle: Controlled execution path
quickActions.description: Move approvals from static queue items toward runtime-mediated Linux actions with explicit scope and rollback expectations.

quickActions.title: Assemble ISO demo
quickActions.subtitle: Package all three layers
quickActions.description: Boot Linux first, initialize the runtime intermediary, then launch SolOS as the visible operating layer with documented image assembly steps.

activityFeed.title: Linux base system detected
activityFeed.detail: Runtime intermediary attached to Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS on lcpby-HP-ENVY-TS-17-Notebook-PC with systemd as init, wayland as the active session type, and uptime 4d 22h 41m.

activityFeed.title: Network state mediated
activityFeed.detail: Runtime reports the host as online and can now expose online/offline state as a stable SolOS contract.

activityFeed.title: App registry surfaced
activityFeed.detail: 4 operating-layer modules are now described as registry-backed entries instead of loose shell copy.

approvals.title: Grant controlled access to mediated system services
approvals.description: Allow runtime-core to expose selected session, launcher, and service information through a bounded SolOS contract.
approvals.scope: runtime intermediary -> systemd / desktop session / launchers

approvals.title: Connect signing and wallet actions to explicit mediated brokers
approvals.description: Prepare a wallet broker boundary so signing remains visible, scoped, and never hidden behind generic agent behavior.
approvals.scope: wallet bridge / secure approvals / runtime mediation

apps.name: Workspace
apps.subtitle: Operating layer context
apps.description: Coordinates user tasks, notes, and live environment state above the runtime intermediary.

apps.name: Approval Lane
apps.subtitle: Policy boundary
apps.description: Surfaces runtime-mediated host actions that require explicit consent before execution.

apps.name: Wallet Hub
apps.subtitle: Ownership surface
apps.description: Keeps identity, balances, and signing visible through explicit runtime-mediated flows.

apps.name: Ghost Console
apps.subtitle: Native agent presence
apps.description: Turns runtime-mediated host state into agent-readable, approval-aware orchestration inside SolOS.
